title: Humularia luentensis
taxonomy: Humularia luentensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae. It belongs to the genus Humularia and is a type of legume native to tropical Africa. Humularia luentensis is also classified as a member of the tribe Dialiinae and the subfamily Faboideae. The plant's classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other species. Further research is necessary to confirm the complete classification of Humularia luentensis.

intro: Humularia luentensis, also known as the Luenté tree, is a plant species native to West Africa, belonging to the family Leguminosae. The plant has been traditionally used in West African medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Humularia luentensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Humularia luentensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make herbal teas and tonics, as well as being used in topical applications.
